channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New allegations against Pete Hegseth alarm Democrats as Pentagon confirmation looms
item.description: Senate Democrats are sounding the alarm over a sworn affidavit from the ex-sister-in-law of Defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth that accuses him of being abusive toward his second wife.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump demands an apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday demanded an apology from the Episcopal bishop of Washington after she made a direct appeal to him during a prayer service marking his inauguration to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and migrant workers who are in the United States illegally.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, TIFFANY STANLEY and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration cancels travel for refugees already cleared to resettle in the US
item.description: Refugees who'd been approved to travel to the United States before a Jan. 27 deadline suspending America’s refugee resettlement program have had their travel plans canceled by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri And Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump rescinds Biden's executive order on AI safety in attempt to diverge from his predecessor
item.description: Hours after returning to the White House, President Donald Trump made a symbolic mark on the future of artificial intelligence by repealing former President Joe Biden’s guardrails for the fast-developing technology.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump demands an apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: President Donald Trump is demanding an apology from the Episcopal bishop of Washington after she asked him to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and migrants in the United States illegally during a prayer service marking his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What cabinet appointments for Sean Duffy, Kristi Noem, and Doug Burgum could mean for Minnesota
item.description: Three regional leaders—Doug Burgum, Kristi Noem, and Sean Duffy—are among President-elect Donald Trump’s Cabinet nominees. Their potential appointments could significantly influence Minnesota and neighboring states.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gracie Stockton, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing the risk of sending kids to school
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Estimated 24.6 million TV viewers watched inauguration coverage, smallest audience since 2013
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An estimated 24.6 million television viewers watched President Donald Trump's second inauguration, the smallest audience for the quadrennial ceremony since Barack Obama's second inauguration in 2013.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US adults want border security action but mostly oppose arrests in schools, churches: AP-NORC poll
item.description: An AP-NORC poll finds many U.S. adults support the idea of beefing up security at the southern border and undertaking some deportations.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Thomson-Deveaux And Tim Sullivan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here’s where Trump’s Cabinet nominees stand
item.description: A slate of President Trump’s Cabinet nominees have appeared before senators in recent days for key hearings on their road to confirmation. Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) was the first to be confirmed, securing the secretary of State position with a unanimous vote on inauguration day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, his administration took down the Spanish version of the official White House website.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Figueroa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump finds new ways to flex presidential power after returning to White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is swiftly breaching the traditional boundaries of presidential power as he returns to the White House, bringing to bear a lifetime of bending the limits in courthouses, boardrooms and politics to forge an expansive view of his authority.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Estimated 24.6 million TV viewers watched inauguration coverage, smallest audience since 2013
item.description: The Nielsen Company says an estimated 24.6 million people watched coverage of President Donald Trump's second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signs 'full and unconditional' pardon of Silk Road creator Ross Ulbricht
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday pardoned Ross Ulbricht, founder of the anonymous marketplace website Silk Road, which the president promised to do on Day 1.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Key career officials at Justice Department reassigned to different positions, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has reassigned key senior officials across multiple divisions as part of a leadership shakeup ahead of the expected confirmation of President Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, multiple people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump defends pardoning of violent Jan. 6 offenders
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday defended signing mass pardons for rioters charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, including those who were violent offenders, a move that has drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is moving to end affirmative action in federal contracting and is directing that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Immigration top priority for Trump and Florida lawmakers
item.description: Solving the illegal immigration crisis is a priority for both President Donald Trump and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ramaswamy won't serve on Trump's DOGE as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: Ramaswamy, who sought the Republican nomination for president in 2024, has signaled plans to run for governor of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Migrants left in despair at the border as asylum system shuts down
item.description: The CBP One app has provided nearly 900,000 people with asylum appointments since January 2023, contributing to a drop in illegal border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta and Lexie Schapitl, MPR News

item.title: Texas could join Louisiana with a law to require the Ten Commandments in classrooms
item.description: Similar proposals are in multiple states after a court ruling in 2022, though opponents say the move would impose one religious view on people with different religious traditions
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider / Houston Public Media, MPR News

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams will attend Donald Trump’s inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams will attend President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday in Washington, D.C. Adams added the inauguration to his schedule Monday morning. He canceled existing appearances in New York City, including two celebrations of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's family circle has different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawler: Trump has right to revoke congestion pricing approval
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Congestion pricing began in New York City earlier this month. Under congestion pricing, drivers are charged a $9 toll during peak hours to enter Manhattan’s central business district at and below 60th Street. U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa mayor meets with other U.S. mayors, responds to proposed conditions on disaster aid
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor was in Washington D.C. this weekend for the U.S. Conference of Mayors’ winter meeting. It's a time when hundreds of city leaders unite to discuss challenges and solutions involving affordable housing, infrastructure, immigration, and the climate.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court aims for fall trial date in sexual misconduct lawsuit against Fletcher
item.description: The lawsuit facing Nathan Fletcher over allegations of sexual misconduct could move to a civil trial as soon as fall 2025, as both sides continue to bump heads over text messages between the ex-supervisor's accuser and a friend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
